title: Office Administrator
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Office Administrator- Sydney. About Us – Greenbox Architecture. Founded in 2009, Greenbox Architecture is a leading Australian design practice in architecture and interior design. We specialise in mission-critical architecture for data centres, health, and life sciences, alongside a robust interiors team focusing on commercial and workplace design. Our portfolio ranges from $400k to over $3 billion, serving startups to global industry leaders. We have a valuable mix of skills within our team including architects, strategists, interior designers, project coordinators and BIM (Revit) technicians. Driven by our love of experiencing and learning new things, Greenbox invests generously in nourishing the passion and development of our team. Our diverse team of over 70 members speaks over 30 languages, bringing a wealth of experience and creativity. In 2025, we set up our Melbourne office to respond to an increased demand in VIC. We want others to enjoy working with us – we always have fun. Our certification by ‘Great Place To Work’, a global authority on workplace culture, is evidence of this. In October 2024, Greenbox joined the Woolpert team. With over 2, employees and more than 60 offices across the U.S., Canada, Europe, South Africa, and Asia-Pacific, Woolpert has an active presence across the world and are able to deliver more exciting and innovative designs in a diverse market. www.greenboxarchitecture.com. Office Administrator. Join Our Creative Team as an Office Administrator!. Are you highly organised, proactive, and eager to play a key role in a vibrant architectural studio? We’re looking for an Office Administrator to be the heartbeat of our Sydney office, keeping the practice running seamlessly and supporting a passionate, creative team. This is a part-time opportunity (4 days per week), ideal for someone seeking balance while still wanting to be deeply involved in a dynamic, design-led environment. What You’ll Be Doing. This is a hands-on, varied role where no two days are the same. You’ll manage the -day rhythm of the office, from handling calls and emails with professionalism to coordinating calendars, meetings, and travel arrangements. You’ll keep meeting rooms presentation- ready, maintain well-organised project files, and ensure the office and kitchen are stocked and running efficiently for both everyday work and important client meetings. You’ll also support administrative and financial processes, including ordering supplies, processing invoices, tracking expenses, and assisting with document management. Working closely with the Practice Manager, you’ll provide essential support to architects and project managers, helping ensure the studio operates smoothly and efficiently. The Ideal Candidate. You’re someone who thrives on organisation, takes initiative, and enjoys being the support person in a busy office. You bring strong communication skills, excellent attention to detail, and confidence using Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook). Experience in an architectural or design studio is highly regarded but not essential. What matters most is your reliability, adaptability, and positive attitude. This role is perfectly suited to someone who can work four days a week and is looking for a long-term, meaningful position within a creative practice. Why You’ll Love Working With Us. You’ll be part of a collaborative, welcoming, and creative team where your contribution genuinely matters. We offer a supportive studio culture, exposure to exciting architectural projects, and a competitive salary and benefits, pro-rated for part-time hours. If you’re ready to be the backbone of a thriving design studio while enjoying a balanced work week, we’d love to hear from you. and help keep our studio thriving. #Greenbox. title: Project Engineer / Task Leader - Water Resources
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Position Overview Woolpert is hiring a Project Engineer/Task Leader to join our dynamic Water Team in South Carolina. This position is primarily responsible for assisting with technical delivery on projects as well as the budgeting, planning, scheduling, monitoring, and reporting for specific project phase(s) and/or task(s). This position is key in establishing and improving levels of team technical competence, quality, and efficiency. This position will be part of a team engaging clients across the water markets in support of client objectives in the areas of Data Analytics, Asset Management, Organizational Performance, Permit Compliance, Process Improvement, and related studies and analyses. This position will perform other duties as assigned. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country where the job is located. What You Will Do:. Support various phases of our water/wastewater/stormwater management projects, from communication with clients to deliverable generation. Perform technical aspects of specified deliverables and/or lead a team of technical staff in creating deliverables consistent with scope, schedule, and budget. Manage phases of projects, ensuring completion of work that meets quality assurance requirements. Perform field work, as needed. Analyze and interpret information, including field and water quality data, to present to clients in a meaningful format. Meet internal and/or external project deadlines. Comply with and implement clearly defined QA/QC measures. Communicate issues affecting phase/task or project schedule, budget, or quality and escalate unresolved issues to the project manager, team leader, and/or discipline leader. Support the development and advancement of stormwater design projects with consideration of local regulatory requirements and design standards. Analyze and interpret stormwater model results for project development and capital improvement program planning. What You Will Bring:. Bachelor’s degree in Civil, Biosystems, or a related engineering program. Minimum of 6 years of relevant professional experience. Willingness to perform technical tasks as assigned, as well as ensure the completion of additional tasks by other team members. Strong communication skills, both written and oral. Strong organizational skills to successfully manage multiple, competing priorities. Ability to interface effectively with clients, team members, and other firm resources. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. Ability to make sound decisions/recommendations based on the evaluation and synthesis of project information and data. Physical and mental capability to perform all essential duties required of the position. Task and personnel management experience is preferred. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. Woolpert is hiring a Senior Project Architect to join our dynamic Architecture team in our Virginia Beach, VA office. This position reports to the Architectural Team Leader and is responsible for contributing to multiple projects types across the region and across the nation. Project types include lower/higher educational facilities, data centers, and other public or private market developments. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. #LI-SN1. What You Will Do:. Drive project execution, along with the project manager, working closely with clients, consultants, team members, and other project contributors. Guide and manage a team of architects, engineers, and production staff to deliver innovative projects that exceed client expectations. Lead research into relevant code, materials and systems, and client-specific prompts, leveraging derived insights to create unique and innovative design solutions. Develop thoughtful technical solutions and manage the delivery of clear and concise project documentation. Seek opportunities to contribute to design excellence by playing an active role in a collaborative design process. Participate in the conceptual design of projects and provide full support through the entire project life cycle. Develop and represent lead technical viewpoint on projects. Review completed reports, plans, estimates, and calculations. Prepare graphics, presentations, contract drawings, and regulatory submissions. Organize and maintain project record information and drawings. Coordinate with clients, project consultants, and engineers. Plan and supervise design and/or technical work of less senior architects and technicians. What You Will Bring:. Minimum of 15 years’ experience in the design of architectural projects. Bachelor’s degree in architecture; master’s degree a plus. Architectural License or NCARB certification required; LEED accreditation preferred. Proficient in Revit and Bluebeam with advanced-level competency in Microsoft Office Suite. Ability to apply architectural principles to buildings/structures and their components or assemblies. Ability to handle designing and drafting assignments including site plans, floor plans, elevations, and sections required. Self-motivated to meet deadlines and improve process and deliverables. Excellent communication skills required. Ability to write reports and specifications required. Benefits You Will Love. title: Survey Co-op - Fall 2026
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Overview. Seeking a new career challenge? Then Woolpert is seeking you! Woolpert is hiring a Survey Technician Intern. This position reports to Management Consulting leadership. The Survey Technician Intern will work alongside Woolpert Survey field technicians collecting and processing survey data. Some projects could be scanning, Topos-Boundary, Photo Control (Fly over points set on the ground). All of these projects directly impact the communities where we live and work. Some projects could be assisting with GIS, completing transportation PASER analysis, performing aggregate depth analysis and data collection. This is a full-time internship for the Fall 2026 (Aug-Dec 2026) season which can be done in Dayton, Columbus or Cincinnati, OH. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What you will do:. Work as part of the integrated Survey field team. Gain real-world experience collecting data in the field with licensed surveyors. Assist with the collection and processing of data for clients. Perform tasks and solve problems with a high degree of independence. What you will bring:. Don’t have all of these? That’s ok, we know you’re coming to learn. Apply if you think this can be a good fit. Be enrolled as a student at an accredited college or university, preferably with at least sophomore status. Be studying Surveying or related field. Be willing to work outside in the field in all types of weather conditions. Have a general understanding and working knowledge of various Survey equipment including Trimble Business Center Software, Microstation, and AutoCAD is preferred. Have strong written and verbal communication skills. Be a self- motivated student. Have exceptional multitasking and time management skills. Be willing to travel when needed through the duration of the internship. Why Woolpert?. Woolpert is looking for supportive, goal-oriented, and career-minded individuals who are, or are looking to become, industry leaders in their fields. We blend design excellence with innovative technology and geospatial applications to deliver exceptional value to our clients. When you join us, you'll join a team of experts who are passionate about their work and dedicated to building the generation of industry leaders. Woolpert embraces progress and innovation, creating limitless opportunities for your career growth. title: Senior Civil Engineering Project Manager - Aviation
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Position Overview. Woolpert is hiring a Senior Engineer Project Manager to join our dynamic Aviation Team. This position reports to our Aviation leadership team and is responsible for leading and managing the design, planning, and delivery of aviation and airfield improvement projects. This role oversees technical document preparation, directs multidisciplinary teams, coordinates with stakeholders such as TxDOT and airport sponsors, and ensures projects are delivered within scope, schedule, and budget. The ideal candidate brings strong civil engineering expertise, robust project management experience, and familiarity with FAA regulations and advisory circulars. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. Qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $8,000 based on experience and role alignment. Bonus eligibility is contingent upon 12 months of service and must be repaid if the employee leaves the company within one year of hire. What You Will Do:. Project Leadership & Management. Provide strategic and technical leadership for aviation and airfield engineering projects from conception through completion. Lead the development of construction plans, technical specifications, contract documents, and specialized engineering reports in alignment with FAA, TxDOT, and sponsor requirements. Establish clearly defined project scopes, prepare detailed fee proposals, and proactively manage project budgets, schedules, and resources to ensure successful and timely delivery. Monitor project performance using earned value metrics, milestone tracking, and forecasting tools, identifying risks early and implementing effective mitigation strategies. Ensure consistency and quality across deliverables through structured QA/QC processes and adherence to applicable engineering standards, FAA Advisory Circulars, and best practices in airfield planning, pavement design, grading, geometry, and safety areas. Facilitate kickoff meetings, progress reviews, design workshops, and internal coordination sessions to maintain alignment across stakeholders and team members. Prepare and deliver accurate monthly invoicing, financial updates, and comprehensive Project Status Reports (PSRs) that outline progress, issues, and recommended actions. Provide mentorship, guidance, and technical oversight to project engineers and designers, supporting professional development and strengthening the aviation group’s overall technical capabilities. Stakeholder Coordination & Communication. Serve as the primary point of contact for airport sponsors, TxDOT, and key stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. Coordinate meetings, prepare agendas and schedules, document decisions, and ensure clear and timely communication to maintain project alignment. Build and maintain positive client relationships through proactive engagement, responsiveness, and consistent follow-through. Documentation & Compliance. Review sponsor-provided documents, including as-built drawings and prior design reports. Coordinate with subconsultants for surveying, geotechnical investigations, environmental studies, and specialized technical services. Ensure project documentation complies with federal, state, and local requirements, including FAA grant processes, environmental regulations, and permitting rules. Bidding & Construction Support. Manage the bidding process by organizing pre-bid meetings, issuing addenda, responding to contractor inquiries, and evaluating proposals. Provide construction-phase support through site visits, review of change orders, coordination with contractors, and monitoring of field activities to uphold quality standards and design intent. Project Close-Out. Review and approve final engineering drawings, compile project cost summaries, and participate in final inspections with TxDOT and project sponsors. Ensure all close-out documentation is delivered accurately and in accordance with sponsor and regulatory requirements. Client & Team Management. Lead cross-functional internal and external teams through project execution. Strengthen client relationships by identifying opportunities during proposals, RFP/RFQ processes, and ongoing engagements. Partner with management to evaluate client satisfaction, manage project risks, and support the overall financial health of the aviation practice. Provide direction, mentorship, and performance support to junior staff. What You Will Bring:. Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering. Professional Engineer (PE) license required. 10+ years of project management experience in aviation and airfield design. Familiarity with FAA Advisory Circulars and federal aviation regulations preferred. Proficiency in AutoDesk Civil 3D, AutoCAD, and related engineering software. Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Strong analytical and technical reading skills. Ability to respond professionally to sensitive client inquiries. Ability to sit for extended periods; occasional reaching, bending, and lifting up to 40 pounds. Occasional travel required for site visits and client meetings. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. #LI-Hybrid.. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Pay Range. $139,200 - $174,000 USD. title: Engineer in Training, Roadway Design
raw_description: Woolpert is hiring an experienced Roadway Engineer (EIT) to join our dynamic Transportation Design team in one of our Ohio offices. This position will offer the opportunity to work on a variety of transportation projects spanning multiple states, multiple clients and presenting unique challenges. Our Team is staffed with industry experts who are passionate about mentoring the generation of designers and teaching vital skills for becoming a successful engineer. Given the wide range of projects and clients, our engineers are exposed to a number of different practices which allow them to not only become well rounded, but also to find a specialty they have interest in. While work is usually related to transportation, engineers regularly work in disciplines outside of the standard transportation roles. These can include work with aviation, military, private development as well as bikeways and trails! Working side by side with proven experts and professionals will establish a very strong base to build off of and allow for growth. This Team is expanding with additional expansion expected over the few years. This allows for lots of potential for upward mobility once experience has been gained! Bonus eligibility is contingent upon 12 months of service and must be repaid if the employee leaves the company within one year of hire. What You Will Do:. Working for and under the advisement of Licensed Professionals to fulfil civil and transportation tasks. Assist with design and production of roadway, bikeway, pedestrian, site civil and transportation planning projects. Work in several computer programs, following training, to perform engineering and design tasks. Apply knowledge to calculations pertaining to design. Aid in the design of 3D models for projects. title: Sanitary Sewer Vac Truck Driver - Expression of Interest
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Overview. There is no immediate need for this role, however we see these kinds of opportunities on a regular basis and welcome folks to express interest. The Jet-Vac Operator position is responsible for cleaning of storm water and sanitary sewers, and occasional diving in sanitary manholes. The candidate is required to hold a valid Commercial Driver’s License (CDL) and must be able to learn and adapt to Woolpert specific processes and procedures. This position may include occasional travel. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located.. Responsibilities. Perform robotic telemonitoring inspections of sewer lines per NASSCO standards through use of robotic CCTV camera and performing advanced field data collection and troubleshooting for a variety of water-resource-related projects. Operate Vac Truck and maintain CCTV equipment. Performs all work tasks required to support execution of sewer maintenance, inspection and/or rehabilitation pipeline tasks. Operation and maintenance of all equipment. Assist in the cleaning of sewer lines as needed. Follows all company and OSHA mandated safety policies and procedures. Follows local and federal DOT regulation while operating machinery and vehicles. Performs other duties as assigned.. Qualifications. Class A or Class B CDL. Ability to work independently with minimal supervision. Knowledge of company policies and procedures. Knowledge of OSHA regulations. Safe driving record. Excellent attention to details. Works well in a team environment. Must be able to lift 50lbs. regularly. Physical position working outdoors requiring standing, walking, climbing, kneeling and crouching at least 50% of the time. High School diploma or GED. Possession of a current, appropriate and valid driver's license. NASSCO PACP, LACP, and MACP certified preferred but not required. Physical Requirements. Ability to frequently lift and move more than pounds. Must be able to frequently stoop, kneel, crouch and crawl. Must be able to frequently reach with hands and arms and climb. Ability to climb down a ladder into manhole with safety gear to access work locations. Must have vision abilities of close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.. title: Senior Project Architect
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Woolpert is hiring a Project Architect to join our dynamic Architecture team in our Coral Gables, FL or Ft. Lauderdale, FL office. This position reports to the Architectural Team Leader and is responsible for contributing to multiple projects types across the region and across the nation. Project types include industrial, manufacturing, aviation and other public or private market developments. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. #LI-SN1. What You Will Do:. Drive project execution, along with the project manager, working closely with clients, consultants, team members, and other project contributors. Guide and manage a team of architects, engineers, and production staff to deliver innovative projects that exceed client expectations. Lead research into relevant code, materials and systems, and client-specific prompts, leveraging derived insights to create unique and innovative design solutions. Develop thoughtful technical solutions and manage the delivery of clear and concise project documentation. Seek opportunities to contribute to design excellence by playing an active role in a collaborative design process. Participate in the conceptual design of projects and provide full support through the entire project life cycle. Develop and represent lead technical viewpoint on projects. Review completed reports, plans, estimates, and calculations. Prepare graphics, presentations, contract drawings, and regulatory submissions. Organize and maintain project record information and drawings. Coordinate with clients, project consultants, and engineers. Plan and supervise design and/or technical work of less senior architects and technicians. What You Will Bring:. Minimum of 15 years’ experience in the design of architectural projects. Bachelor’s degree in architecture; master’s degree a plus. Architectural License or NCARB certification required; LEED accreditation preferred. Proficient in Revit and Bluebeam with advanced-level competency in Microsoft Office Suite. Ability to apply architectural principles to buildings/structures and their components or assemblies. Ability to handle designing and drafting assignments including site plans, floor plans, elevations, and sections required. Self-motivated to meet deadlines and improve process and deliverables. Excellent communication skills required. Ability to write reports and specifications required. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Pay Range. $101,200 - $126,500 USD. We are looking for a Senior Project Engineer to support our South Region team, a crucial role geared towards the design and management of transportation infrastructure projects, mainly for TxDOT. This individual will be integral in steering our team towards the delivery of exceptional schematic designs, PS&E, structural plans, and precise cost estimations. Only candidates located in Texas will be considered. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located.. What you will do:. Lead and collaborate with a team to manage and execute bridge design tasks for diverse transportation projects, focusing predominantly on TxDOT projects. Oversee the preparation of comprehensive structural designs, specifications, and cost estimations for a variety of structures, including bridges, retaining walls, and other related infrastructure. Supervise load rating analysis, rehabilitation concepts and structure type analysis evaluations. Mentor engineering team to use current engineering design software to complete designs. Coordinate with other design teams and disciplines. Offer technical guidance to Professional Engineer/EIT and CAD Designer / Drafters on project. Develop design procedures to facilitate high-quality cost-effective work by others. Participates in interdisciplinary review of project deliverables. Develops construction cost estimates and estimates of technical efforts for projects. Execute quality assurance checks on design calculations and drawings. Develop engineering budget and schedule to support project management.. What you will bring:. Bachelor’s degree in Engineering and 6+ years of relevant experience OR Master’s degree in Engineering and 5+ years of relevant experience. Texas PE license is required. Knowledge of industry standard computer software such as PGSuper, WinCore, LEAP Bridge Concrete, MicroStation, spColumn, LPile, MDX, CSiBridge, STAAD PRO, AASHTOWare etc. A track record of successfully leading bridge design projects. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. Benefits You Will Love. title: Transportation Co-op - Fall 2026
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Overview. Seeking an exciting opportunity to launch or grow your career? Then Woolpert is seeking you! Join us for an internship experience that offers real-world project involvement within a supportive environment alongside ample opportunities for professional and personal growth. At this time, we are seeking multiple Transportation Engineering Interns in our Ohio (Dayton, Columbus, Cincinnati and Cleveland) offices for Fall 2026 (Aug-Dec 2026). This internship will be a full-time position working 40 hours a week. The Transportation Engineering Intern will work alongside the Woolpert infrastructure teams working on a variety of projects across the Transportation spectrum including projects in traffic, roadway and pedestrian facilities, bridges and structures and transportation planning. Work can include several disciplines across a variety of clients and project types. All these projects directly impact the communities where we live and work. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What you will do:. Work as part of the industry leading Transportation team. Gain real-world experience working on actual projects alongside Licensed Engineers that are experts in their fields. Assist on a variety of projects doing such things as analysis, design, plan production and cost estimation. Use industry leading applications such as Civil 3D and OpenRoads Designer, alongside discipline specific programs that aid in design. Work as a member of a team that coordinates with other internal teams and external subconsultants and clients. Perform tasks and solve problems with a high degree of independence. What you will bring:. Don’t have all of these? That’s ok, we know you’re coming to learn. Apply if you think this can be a good fit. Be enrolled as a student at an accredited college or university, preferably with at least sophomore status. Pursuing a Civil Engineering degree or civil adjacent degree. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Ability to function well in a collaborative, team-oriented environment. Exceptional multitasking and time management skills. Be ready and open to learning!. Be located in Ohio near one of our offices or be willing to relocate at your own expense as this position requires at least 2 days/week of in office time. Level up your career with Woolpert. and join our team to help shape the future of tomorrow!. Why Woolpert?. Woolpert, Inc. is a cutting-edge national architecture, engineering and geospatial (AEG) firm that delivers value to clients by blending site and architectural design excellence with innovative technology and geospatial applications. Woolpert is certified as an Employer of Choice by Great Place To Work®. We believe great companies are built from exceptional people and amazing clients. Woolpert is looking for supportive, goal-oriented, and career-minded individuals who are, or are looking to become, industry leaders in their fields. We blend design excellence with innovative technology and geospatial applications to deliver exceptional value to our clients. When you join us, you'll join a team of experts who are passionate about their work and dedicated to building the generation of industry leaders. Woolpert embraces progress and innovation, creating limitless opportunities for your career growth. Our vision:. raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Position Overview. Woolpert is hiring a Civil Engineering CAD Technician to join our dynamic Site Civil Team. This position will report to the Engineering Team Leader and will support hybrid or in office in our St. Louis, MO office. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. #LI-SN1. What You Will Do as a Civil Engineering CAD Technician at Woolpert:. Design and create site layout plans in alignment with client requirements and Woolpert standards. Utilize AutoCAD Civil 3D to develop concept plans, grading plans, and site designs. Work with project management teams to identify impediments and develop/implement solutions to overcome obstacles. Perform site due diligence assessments and compile data and reports. Perform roadway pavement evaluations and compile reports. Perform occasional field work, frequent local travel, and overnight travel, as needed. What You Will Bring:. Must have a n Associates degree or technical certification from a technical schoo l or four (4) years related experience. Experience in Autodesk Civil 3D. Valid Driver’s License – Clean driving record. Strong interest and working knowledge in hydrology and stormwater management. Proven ability to multi-task, appropriately prioritize workflow, and complete tasks. Must be mentally and physically capable of performing all essential duties required of the position. Must have above average verbal and written communication skills for both internal and external relationships. Must be able to effectively communicate and present information in -one and small group situations. Must be able to read and interpret construction drawings and specifications. Must be able to read, write and interpret reports, business correspondence and procedural documentation. Must be able to apply mathematical concepts of plane and solid geometry and trigonometry. Must be able to perform basic mathematical calculations (fractions, percentages, ratios and proportions) and apply to practical situations. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Pay Range. $51,100 - $63,900 USD. This position reports to the Geospatial Team Leader and is responsible for supporting our Geospatial teams with Surveying and Data Processing. This is a full-time, 40 hours per week, paid internship for Summer 2026. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What you will do:*. Assisting with the acquisition and processing of hydrographic and geophysical data in support of government organizations and private sector clients for services such as nautical charting, dredging, marine construction, cable route mapping, pipeline inspection, and more. Engaging in data quality checks and preliminary analysis. Learning and applying various hydrographic software tools and methodologies. Contributing to the development and implementation of data management strategies. Supporting the team in ensuring data accuracy and integrity. *Intern responsibilities vary based on team placement within each department geographically. What you will bring:. A strong desire to learn new skills, including technical specifications and software. Excellent organizational capabilities. Efficient time management skills. Ability to grasp new information in challenging situations. Open to receiving and acting on guidance and feedback from mentors. Strong interpersonal skills and a commitment to teamwork. Candidate Requirements:. A general understanding of hydrographic surveys. Physical ability and willingness to work on 20 to 70ft vessels in coastal environments. Depending on intern placement, willing and able to spend up to 2-3 weeks in the field for data collection while also completing rotations in an office to support processing. A proactive attitude towards learning and using new software tools. Presenting Opportunities and Challenges at Every Turn. As a firm that recognizes the importance of developing top talent from within, our employees have access to a wide range of training and coaching programs and are rewarded for their achievements through our excellent benefits package and competitive salaries. Mid-level Project Architect/Designer - Sydney. About Us – Greenbox Architecture (A Woolpert Company). Founded in 2009, Greenbox Architecture is a leading Australian design practice in architecture and interior design. We specialise in mission-critical architecture for data centres, health, and life sciences, alongside a robust interiors team focusing on commercial and workplace design. Our portfolio ranges from $400k to over $3 billion, serving startups to global industry leaders. We have a valuable mix of skills within our team including architects, strategists, interior designers, project coordinators and BIM (Revit) technicians. Driven by our love of experiencing and learning new things, Greenbox invests generously in nourishing the passion and development of our team. Our diverse team of over 80 members speaks over 30 languages, bringing a wealth of experience and creativity. In 2025, we set up our Melbourne office to respond to an increased demand in VIC. We want others to enjoy working with us – we always have fun. Our certification by ‘Great Place To Work’, a global authority on workplace culture, is evidence of this. In October 2024, Greenbox joined the Woolpert team. With over 2, employees and more than 60 offices across the U.S., Canada, Europe, South Africa, and Asia-Pacific, Woolpert has an active presence across the world and are able to deliver more exciting and innovative designs in a diverse market. www.greenboxarchitecture.com. Mid-level Project Architect/Designer -Sydney. Join a dynamic Sydney team shaping the future of global industries and be a critical member of the growth of our Sydney team. We are leaders in high-demand sectors with a strong growth trajectory, offering exciting opportunities for career development. Our collaborative approach and values make us a preferred partner among clients and colleagues alike. We are expanding and seek experienced mid-level architect/designer to contribute to large-scale projects nationally and globally. About You:. We seek a mid-level project architect/designer with experience of complex projects, a solid understanding of design development and delivery, adept at problem-solving and collaboration with team members and consultants for optimal project outcomes. This is a role with a huge growth potential. You will be joining an emerging team of architects and designers in our Sydney office You will play a crucial part of the growth in the market. Key Responsibilities:. Capable of working on complex, multi-phase projects with a focus on achieving high-quality outcomes and compliance with regulations. Prepare architectural drawings, models that accurately reflect the design intent. Assist in ensuring designs and technical documentation are compliant with project requirements and standards under supervision of senior staff. Communicates design concepts effectively to clients and stakeholders using strong visual and verbal presentation skills. Ideal Candidate Profile:. Minimum 5 years of local experience. Has a good understanding of design and construction documentation. Has a solid understanding of Australian building codes. Strong level of technical/construction knowledge is highly regarded. Experience working in complex commercial project or large scale multi-residential projects is highly regarded. Capacity to work as part of a team with the ability to work autonomously as required. Revit proficiency is essential. Rendering (Enscape, Lumion) and Photoshop skills will also be highly regarded. #Greenbox. #onsite. We want others to enjoy working with us – we always have fun. Our certification by ‘Great Place To Work’, a global authority on workplace culture, is evidence of this. In October 2024, Greenbox joined the Woolpert team. With over 2, employees and more than 60 offices across the U.S., Canada, Europe, South Africa, and Asia-Pacific, Woolpert has an active presence across the world and are able to deliver more exciting and innovative designs in a diverse market. www.greenboxarchitecture.com. Senior Project Architect/Designer – Sydney. Join a dynamic Sydney team shaping the future of global industries. We are leaders in high-demand sectors with a strong growth trajectory, offering exciting opportunities for career development. Our collaborative approach and values make us a preferred partner among clients and colleagues alike. We are seeking experienced senior architect/designer to continue their career growth and contribute to large-scale projects nationally and globally. About You:. We seek a senior project architect/designer with experience of all phases of complex commercial projects, a solid understanding of design development and delivery, adept at problem-solving and collaboration with team members and consultants for optimal project outcomes. Key Responsibilities:. Delivers design and documentation required throughout each phase of the project lifecycle, with the team and under the guidance of Directors. Skilled in design development, technical drawing, and project coordination. Demonstrates expertise in various aspects of design/documentation or a good understanding of all aspects, including concept development, space planning, material selection, and construction documentation. Capable of working on and leading parts of complex, multi-phase projects with a focus on achieving high-quality outcomes and compliance with regulations. Prepare architectural drawings, models that accurately reflect the design intent. Assist in ensuring designs and technical documentation are compliant with project requirements and standards under supervision of senior staff. Take the lead on major presentations and negotiations. Communicates design concepts effectively to clients and stakeholders using strong visual and verbal presentation skills. Ideal Candidate Profile:. Minimum 8 years of local experience. Has a good understanding of design and construction documentation through the life cycle of the project. Has a solid understanding of the NCC, Australian Standards and relevant industry regulations. Strong level of technical/construction knowledge is highly regarded. Experience working in complex commercial project is required. Capacity to work as part of a team as well as lead a team under the guidance of directors. Revit proficiency is essential. raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Overview. No immediate vacancy, but we are always on the lookout for talented students to join our Aviation engineering team. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. This position focuses on aviation and airfield infrastructure projects, providing real-world experience alongside licensed professionals. Under the guidance of Professional Engineers, the intern will support all phases of airport design and construction, from planning and analysis to on-site fieldwork.. Responsibilities. Assist in the development of airfield design documents using AutoCAD and Civil 3D. Perform engineering calculations, design analyses, and prepare technical reports under the supervision of a Professional Engineer. Contribute to the preparation of construction documents, cost estimates, and FAA-compliant designs. Participate in field visits and inspections to observe ongoing construction and verify quality standards. Collaborate with cross-disciplinary teams to deliver projects on scope, schedule, and budget. Support planning and preliminary design efforts for runway, taxiway, and various other airport infrastructure projects. Analyze data, apply standard engineering techniques, and identify discrepancies in results. Maintain and organize project documentation in compliance with FAA and municipal standards. Engage in quality control efforts and assist in resolving technical issues during design or construction phases.. Qualifications. Currently pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering. Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, Microsoft Office Suite. Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to work independently. Willingness to work collaboratively with engineers, project managers, and field staff. Ability to participate in multiple site visits and interact with clients and contractors. title: Bridge Project Manager
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Overview. Woolpert is seeking a Project Manager to join our growing Transportation Team in Texas. The Project Manager will primarily lead the execution of projects for our Texas clients from proposal through detailed design. Ideal candidates will be self-motivated with a strong technical background to guide staff through projects. The Project Manager may also serve as a subject matter expert in support of other projects across the country and around the world. What you will do:. Lead project teams through the development of preliminary engineering, detailed design, and quantification of transportation projects. Ensure deliverables and quality standards are met in accordance with the agreed scope, schedule, and budget. Manage and support internal and client cross-functional teams. Leverage existing relationships and new client and consultant relationships in the pursuit of new work. Actively participate in professional societies, organizations, and conferences by serving on boards, presenting at luncheons or conferences. What you will bring:. Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from an ABET-accredited program preferred. Active PE License in Texas or ability to obtain a Texas PE license within in one year of hire. TXDOT Prequalification’s Bridge Design. Multi-Level Interchange Design. Bridge Class Culvert, Non-Bridge Class Culvert and Inlet Design. Minimum 10 years of experience in bridge design preferred. Must be able to travel 10-15% to client sites. Knowledge of TXDOT Project Development Process, construction documentation & administration and knowledge in state, local municipalities design process is a plus. TXDOT precertification’s in TXDOT bridge design/development preferred. AutoCAD and Civil3D experience preferred. Strong leadership, organization, and communication skills. Ability to work independently as well as in a collaborative team environment; offer constructive feedback and direction to support goals. Woolpert, Inc. is a cutting-edge national architecture, engineering and geospatial (AEG) firm. Our transportation practice is deeply rooted in the firm’s long history of providing exceptional client service and innovative design. Through our integrated design approach, we provide comprehensive, full-service consulting to turn our client problems and needs into opportunities and successes, blending engineering excellence with progressive technology and geospatial applications. We employ seasoned industry leaders who are willing to share their technical expertise to present viable solutions to our clients. Our Transportation group is rapidly becoming one of the top firms serving ODOT and LPA clients across Ohio. Woolpert is certified as an Employer of Choice by Great Place To Work®. We believe great companies are built from exceptional people and amazing clients. title: Vac Truck Driver - Utilities Field Services
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. JOB SUMMARY:. Safely operates industrial vacuum trucks and related equipment to perform environmental cleaning, waste removal, and material transport activities. Supports routine and emergency response operations while adhering to company, state, and federal safety regulations. Works under direct supervision and is expected to follow established procedures and training. JOB DUTIES:. Operate vacuum trucks and related equipment for industrial cleaning, tank cleaning, spill cleanup, and material removal. Assist with setup, operation, and breakdown of hoses, pumps, and tools needed for each job. Load, transport, and unload liquid and solid waste materials in compliance with DOT, OSHA, and environmental regulations. Perform pre-trip and post-trip vehicle inspections; report any defects or maintenance needs. Maintain accurate logs, manifests, and service documentation. Follow all safety protocols, including use of required personal protective equipment (PPE). Support crew members during field operations, including confined space entry, high-pressure washing, or site cleanup. Maintain cleanliness and readiness of vehicles, equipment, and work area. Participate in training and safety meetings as required. Perform other duties as assigned. TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS:. Travel may be required to various work sites or locations as needed. REQUIRED EDUCATION:. High school diploma or equivalent required. REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:. experience in vac truck operation or similar equipment. REQUIRED CERTIFICATIONS, LICENSES, OR TRAINING: (if applicable). Valid Commercial Driver’s License required (Class B at minimum). REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:. Basic knowledge of vehicle and equipment operation. Understanding of safety practices and PPE requirements. Ability to follow written and verbal instructions. Ability to perform physical labor. Dependability and punctuality. Ability to maintain required documentation. Willingness to learn. PREFERRED EDUCATION:. Post-secondary vocational or technical coursework preferred. PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:. Experience in a comparable industry or environment preferred. PREFERRED CERTIFICATIONS, LICENSES, OR TRAINING:. OSHA 40-Hour HAZWOPER Certification preferred. PREFERRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:. Ability to learn and adapt quickly. Problem-solving skills. Good time management and organizational skills. Basic mechanical aptitude. PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS & WORKING CONDITIONS:. Lift to 50 lbs regularly. Stand, walk, climb, or work in confined spaces for extended periods. Work in varied weather and environmental conditions. Industrial, construction, and outdoor job sites. Exposure to loud noise, chemicals, waste materials, and high-traffic areas. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Pay Range. $25 - $26 USD. title: Marine Survey Equipment & Operations Specialist
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth. Position Overview. Woolpert is hiring a Marine Survey Equipment & Operations Specialist to join our dynamic Maritime Group at our Port Orchard, WA office. This role is centered on full lifecycle ownership of marine survey equipment (≈60%) combined with project coordination and financial tracking (≈40%). You will ensure that equipment is maintained, organized, and deployed efficiently while supporting project setup, tracking, and invoicing to keep work moving smoothly from kickoff through closeout. This is a non-travel, office-based role that plays a critical part in enabling successful field operations and maintaining project performance. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What You Will Do:. Equipment Ownership & Logistics (Primary Focus – ~60%). Maintain and manage marine survey equipment inventory, including: Multibeam and singlebeam systems. Side scan sonar, sub-bottom profilers, and magnetometers. GNSS/INS positioning systems and supporting hardware. Coordinate equipment check-in/check-out for field teams and projects. Track equipment status, availability, and utilization. Manage routine maintenance, calibrations, and repairs (RMA process). Coordinate shipping, logistics, and mobilization readiness. Maintain organized storage, documentation, and configuration records. Support continuous improvement of equipment tracking systems and processes. Project Coordination & Financial Tracking (~40%). Set up and manage projects within internal systems for: Opportunity tracking. Budgeting and cost tracking. Labor and expense monitoring. Support invoicing and project billing workflows. Track project financial performance, including: Labor hours. Equipment utilization. Expenses vs. budget. Assist project managers with P&L visibility and cost control. Help ensure projects are accurately tracked from initiation through closeout. Identify gaps or inefficiencies in workflows and recommend improvements. What You Will Bring:. 3–7+ years of experience in: Marine survey, geospatial, or technical equipment environment OR. Equipment logistics, operations coordination, or similar role. Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple equipment assets and projects simultaneously. Experience with inventory tracking, logistics, or asset management systems. Working knowledge of project tracking, invoicing, or financial systems. Detail-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability. Ability to communicate effectively with field teams, project managers, and leadership. Preferred Experience. Familiarity with marine survey equipment (multibeam, side scan, GNSS, etc.). Experience with tools such as: QINSy, CARIS, SonarWiz, or similar (awareness level is fine). Experience with project accounting, budgeting, or ERP systems. Background supporting operations across multiple concurrent projects. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Pay Range. $65,800 - $82,300 USD. title: Senior Manager Growth Marketing, Paid Search, Canada
raw_description: ABOUT QUINCE. Founded in 2018, Quince was built to challenge the idea that nice things have to cost a lot. Our mission is simple: to make really high quality essentials for really low prices, produced fairly and sustainably. We believe everyone deserves exceptional craftsmanship and timeless design without the traditional markups. Quince is a direct-to-consumer (DTC) model that cuts out middlemen and leverages just-in-time manufacturing to minimize waste and maximize value. Quince is a tech company disrupting the retail industry by putting AI, analytics and automation at the center of everything we do. Our unwavering commitment to excellence and company values guide our teams and actions:. Customer First: We prioritize customer satisfaction in every decision. High Quality: True quality means premium materials and rigorous production standards you can feel good about. Essential Design: We focus on timeless, functional essentials instead of chasing trends. Always a Better Deal: Innovation and transparency ensure value for both customers and partners. Social & Environmental Responsibility: We commit to sustainable materials, ethical production, and fair wages. Quince partners with world-class manufacturers across the globe and serves millions of customers. With strong investor backing and a focus on sustainable growth, we are a company that is rapidly scaling while maintaining a commitment to quality, simplicity, and radical price transparency. OUR TEAM AND SUCCESS. At Quince, you will be part of a high-performing team that is redefining what quality, value, and sustainability mean in modern retail. We are a destination for builders, innovators, and operators to come together and challenge the status quo. Our collective ambition is bold. We are creating an entirely new category and customer experience – one that democratizes luxury and provides high quality products at radically low prices. That mission demands a world-class team committed to excellence. If you are motivated by impact, growth, and purpose, you will find a strong sense of belonging at Quince. THE ROLE. Senior Manager, Growth Marketing, Paid Search. We are seeking a Senior Manager Growth Marketing for Paid Search to join our growing team. The ideal candidate is a self-starter, problem-solver and successful in combining technology and data into best-in-class outcomes. The candidate is energized by solving complex business problems and consistently effective in making high-judgement decisions at rapid pace amidst the frequent ambiguity that comes with charting a course of action with no precedent. Moreover, the ideal candidate is energized by an environment where strategy, innovation and decision-making are intentionally distributed, where candor, speed and data are highly valued and colleagues at all levels hold each other to unusually high standards on behalf of Quince customers. As Senior Manager, Growth Marketing, Paid Search you will build the performance marketing for Canada from 0 to 1. This role will play a major part in accelerating Quince’s growth in Canada. Responsibilities. Own and execute the full performance marketing strategy across paid search - including Google Shopping, Google Search, UAC, ASA, YouTube and Bing. Drive both new customer acquisition and retargeting efforts across web and app. Manage channels -end, from strategic campaign planning, bidding strategy, keyword optimization, A/B testing, and achieving ROAS efficiency targets. Continuously experiment and optimize campaigns by testing new products, formats, and landing pages. Build a strong feedback loop with cross-functional partners to refine strategies and support supply chain planning. Partner with the creative strategy team (creative strategists, video editors, graphic designers) to concept, develop, and launch high-performing ad assets tailored to platform best practices. Work with ad tech and marketing operations teams to automate reporting, improve data visibility, and streamline campaign management, optimization workflows, incrementality testing, attribution, and A/B testing processes. Qualifications. Required:. Role is based in Toronto, with 4 days per week (Monday-Thursday) in office requirement. 8+ years of managing Paid Search programs with large budgets with a focus on Google Text Ads, Google Shopping, Google Search, ASA,UAC and Youtube. Expertise in Google Adwords, Google Ads editor along with experience in Bing Ads. In-depth understanding of Paid Search campaign management and optimization best practices. Strong analytical skills and ability to leverage data to drive decision-making. Be a proactive self-starter who enjoys a fast-paced, results-oriented work environment. Have strong program management and organizational skills, being able to manage multiple projects, deadline and priorities with ease. Test, Learn and Scale mindset. Strong proficiency in Google sheets, Lookr, Sigma and more. Experience with Google Ads scripts. All posted ranges are reflective of base salary and may vary depending upon experience level and location. Bonus and equity may also be provided for eligible roles. Pay Range. $130,000 - $150,000 CAD. WHY QUINCE?. Joining Quince means being part of a mission-driven team reshaping retail. You will work alongside talented colleagues, tackle meaningful challenges, and contribute to building a more sustainable, accessible future for customers and partners alike. EQUAL OPPORTUNITY & HIRING INTEGRITY. Quince provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applications for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran or military status, sexual orientation,. Quince is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities. If you need a. We review all requests individually and will work with you to determine appropriate accommodations on a case-by-case basis. Employment is contingent upon successful completion of a background check. Quince will conduct background checks in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. Security Advisory: Beware of Frauds. At Quince, we're dedicated to recruiting top talent who share our drive for innovation. To safeguard candidates, Quince emphasizes legitimate recruitment practices. Initial communication is primarily via official Quince email addresses and; beware of deviations. Personal data and sensitive information will not be solicited during the application phase. Interviews are conducted via phone, in person, or through the approved platforms Google Meets or Zoom—never via messaging apps or other calling services. Offers are merit-based, communicated verbally, and followed up in writing. If personal information is requested to initiate the hiring process, rest assured it will be through secure and protected means Our mission is simple: to make really high quality essentials for really low prices, produced fairly and sustainably. title: Senior Manager, Sourcing - Soft Home (CN/SEA)
raw_description: ABOUT QUINCE. Founded in 2018, Quince was built to challenge the idea that nice things have to cost a lot. Our mission is simple: to make really high quality essentials for really low prices, produced fairly and sustainably. We believe everyone deserves exceptional craftsmanship and timeless design without the traditional markups. Quince is a direct-to-consumer (DTC) model that cuts out middlemen and leverages just-in-time manufacturing to minimize waste and maximize value. Quince is a tech company disrupting the retail industry by putting AI, analytics and automation at the center of everything we do. Our unwavering commitment to excellence and company values guide our teams and actions:. Customer First: We prioritize customer satisfaction in every decision. High Quality: True quality means premium materials and rigorous production standards you can feel good about. Essential Design: We focus on timeless, functional essentials instead of chasing trends. Always a Better Deal: Innovation and transparency ensure value for both customers and partners. Social & Environmental Responsibility: We commit to sustainable materials, ethical production, and fair wages. Quince partners with world-class manufacturers across the globe and serves millions of customers. With strong investor backing and a focus on sustainable growth, we are a company that is rapidly scaling while maintaining a commitment to quality, simplicity, and radical price transparency. OUR TEAM AND SUCCESS. At Quince, you will be part of a high-performing team that is redefining what quality, value, and sustainability mean in modern retail. We are a destination for builders, innovators, and operators to come together and challenge the status quo. Our collective ambition is bold. We are creating an entirely new category and customer experience – one that democratizes luxury and provides high quality products at radically low prices. That mission demands a world-class team committed to excellence. If you are motivated by impact, growth, and purpose, you will find a strong sense of belonging at Quince. THE ROLE:. Senior Manager, Sourcing - Soft Home (CN/SEA). We are seeking an experienced Sourcing Manager specializing in Bedding to lead our sourcing efforts across China and Southeast Asia (SEA). In this role, you'll play a key part in building and managing supplier relationships to ensure our bedding products (such as premium down and premium down alt comforters, pillows, mattress pads and covers, etc.) meet Quince's high standards for quality, sustainability, and cost-efficiency. If you're passionate about ethical sourcing, vendor development, and optimizing supply chains in a fast-paced DTC environment, this is your opportunity to contribute to a brand that's redefining affordable luxury. Key Responsibilities. Develop and execute sourcing strategies for Bedding products, focusing on CN markets to identify, evaluate, and onboard new vendors while maintaining relationships with existing partners. Negotiate contracts, pricing, and terms with suppliers to achieve cost savings without compromising on quality, sustainability, or ethical standards. Collaborate with design, product development, and operations teams to manage product programs, from fabric/yarn selection to final production, ensuring timelines and specifications are met. Conduct market research on trends in Utility Bedding, materials (e.g., organic cotton, goose, feather, specialties ), and sustainable practices to inform sourcing decisions. Oversee quality control processes, including factory audits, compliance checks for environmental and labor standards, and risk management in the supply chain. Monitor production output, resolve issues related to procurement, and optimize inventory to minimize waste and support our direct-shipping model. Travel to supplier sites in CN as needed to build strong partnerships and ensure alignment with Quince's values of transparency and responsibility. Analyze supplier performance metrics and provide reports to senior leadership on cost efficiencies, sustainability impacts, and opportunities for improvement. Qualifications and Requirements. 7-15 years of experience in sourcing or procurement, with a strong focus on Utility Bedding products (e.g.premium down and premium down alt comforters, pillows, mattress pads and covers, etc) in the home goods industry. Proven track record in vendor development, negotiation, and managing international supply chains, particularly in CN regions. Deep knowledge of textile materials, manufacturing processes, and sustainability practices. Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain Management, Textile Engineering, Business, or a related field; advanced certifications (e.g., in sustainable sourcing) are a plus. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for cross-functional collaboration and building global relationships. The Role- Material Sourcing Manager, Silk. RESPONSIBILITIES:. ● Develop and execute a comprehensive Silk procurement strategy that optimizes cost, quality, and sustainability across all applicable products. ● Build, maintain, and expand a global network of Silk suppliers, including tanneries, rawhide processors, and specialty Silk producers, to ensure reliable and competitive sourcing. ● Partner with Product Development and Merchandising to build and maintain a central Silk materials library with a curated assortment of premium Silks optimized for high quality, product consistency, speed to market, and cross-category application. ● Analyze and monitor the global Silk market, including price trends, supply-demand dynamics, sustainability regulations, and emerging risks, to inform procurement strategies and minimize price volatility. ● Establish and maintain long-term partnerships with key suppliers, negotiating favorable terms and driving value across the supply chain. ● Collaborate with cross-functional teams including sourcing, product development, quality assurance, and merchandising to align material procurement strategies with product requirements and company objectives. ● Identify and evaluate opportunities for cost savings, innovation, and process efficiencies across the Silk value chain. ● Create and implement risk mitigation strategies, including multi-sourcing, forward purchasing, and compliance with responsible sourcing initiatives. ● Lead supplier performance management efforts to ensure quality assurance, compliance with ethical labor standards, and adherence to environmental regulations. ● Provide regular reporting on procurement performance, cost savings, and market conditions. ● Establish, codify, and enforce Silk quality performance standards that ensure consistency across vendors, enable accurate teardown benchmarking, and tie material specifications directly to expected product performance, durability, and customer experience. Proactive self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, results-driven environments. Test-and-learn mentality, embracing experimentation to unlock growth and scale successful strategies. Strong proficiency in Google Sheets, including advanced formulas and experience working with Sigma, Lookr etc. Preferred:. AppLovin and Tik Tok experience preferred. title: Regional Sales Lead, Midwest
raw_description: Quince is looking for a Regional Sales Lead (Midwest) who thrives in a “player-coach” dynamic. This role is a foundational leadership position in our high-growth B2B vertical, responsible for scaling an 8-figure pipeline while personally mentoring a high-performing team. We are looking for someone capable of architecting regional strategy at 30, feet, and then diving into the weeds to help an AE close a complex enterprise deal. This role is a foundational leadership position in our high-growth B2B vertical, responsible for scaling an 8-figure pipeline while personally mentoring a high-performing team. We are looking for someone capable of architecting regional strategy at 30, feet, and then diving into the weeds to help an AE close a complex enterprise deal. This position requires a rare combination of tactical enterprise grit and the leadership intuition necessary to build a world-class sales culture. Our midwest region includes: IA, IL, IN, KS, MI, MN, MO, ND, NE, NM, OK, SD, TX, WI. Responsibilities. Team Leadership & Scaling. Coach & Cultivate: Manage and mentor a team of 5-12 Account Executives, focusing on professional development, tactical closing skills, and performance accountability. Build the Bench: Lead recruitment and onboarding for the Midwest region, owning the full integration of new hires through 30/60/90-day benchmarks. Performance Culture: Run high-energy weekly standups, 1:1s, and regional offsites. You aren’t just managing: you’re setting the pace for standard for excellence. Sales Execution & “Player” Impact”. Drive 8-Figure Growth: Own the regional revenue target by identifying high-value enterprise opportunities and driving net-new pipeline through proactive engagement. In-The-Trenches Support: Join key client meetings and industry events to provide executive presence, help navigate complex procurement cycles, and close deals. CRM Excellence: Maintain “source of truth” integrity in HubSpot; oversee pipeline hygiene, forecasting accuracy, and real-time reporting dashboards. Strategic Evolution. Be the Expert: Deep-dive into Quince’s B2B model, product assortment, customization capabilities to act as the ultimate subject matter expert for both clients and your team. Infrastructure Architect: Constantly iterate on sales playbooks, tech stacks, and team infrastructure to remove friction and accelerate the sales cycle. Qualifications. Experience: 5+ years in Enterprise Sales, with 3+ years of direct people management experience. You should have a proven track record of consistently hitting $5M+ annual quotas. Tech-Forward: Power-user of CRMs (HubSpot preferred) and a “bias for automation.” You should be comfortable leveraging AI tools to help scale prospecting, research and outreach. Operational Agility: Proven ability to thrive in ambiguous, fast-paced environments. You prefer building the playbook over following an outdated one. Analytical Rigor: You manage by the numbers. You can look at a dashboard and immediately identify where a deal is stalling and why. Communication: Exceptional presence, whether you’re pitching a C-suite executive or delivering tough love to a direct report. You deliver the right message, to the right audience, and the right time.
